title: France's national political crisis is taking root locally
subhead: The first round of the municipal elections, marked by low turnout and fragmented landscapes in big cities, confirms the depth of France's political crisis, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: War in Iran: 'Spain's resolve stands in stark contrast to France's stance, which seems more accepting of US-Israeli strikes'
subhead: Historian Laurence Badel analyzes the reasons behind the Spanish government's 'no to war' position and the impact of this decision on the European diplomatic landscape.

title: Iran confirms death of security chief Larijani
subhead: Iran's Supreme National Security Council confirmed the death of 'Martyr Dr. Ali Larijani,' stating that his son and his bodyguards died with him, after Israel announced earlier on Tuesday it had killed him in an air strike.

title: PSG crush Chelsea to reach Champions League quarter-finals
subhead: Thanks to the 3-0 win on Tuesday, Paris Saint-Germain's dream of retaining the Champions League title remains very much alive, a year after winning the trophy for the first time in their history.

title: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
subhead: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.

title: French court examines Yazidi genocide for first time in trial of jihadist
subhead: Sabri Essid is being tried in absentia, charged with 'genocide,' a first in France. Two women from the Iraqi minority will testify, recounting how they were enslaved.

title: 2026 municipal election results: Key takeaways from a first round marked by low turnout
subhead: The second round will be less promising than hoped for the far right, despite significant gains in cities in the southeast, particularly in Marseille. Leading in Paris and Montpellier, the Socialists will need to clarify their positions regarding radical left candidates, who performed well.
